title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upsert, Revisions, and Deletion Semantics · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Disappearance Policy
summary: Every poll inserts duplicates, title edits create new posts, reused GUIDs overwrite unrelated content, disappeared entries are deleted immediately, and revision causes vanish. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go disappearance policy increment in idempotent versioned entry ingestion model. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Treat absence from one finite feed page as weak evidence and apply explicit tombstone, retention, correction, and publisher-delete policy.
Store meaningful normalized revisions with source fetch, field diff, timestamps, and bounded retention instead of overwriting evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Disappearance Policy · Theory Feed Go Disappearance Policy

theory case — A theory Go team handles case 1922 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
